analysisSteps: locate entry points from code, tests, routes, handlers, commands, or failing output
follow the call path across imports, dependency injection, task scheduling, and event dispatch
identify input, state, algorithm, async, external, persistence, cleanup, report, and observability boundaries
declare executor scope before choosing commands: internal-agent, external-agent, dashboard, cli, mcp-public, or mcp-internal
record branches and degradation paths such as skip flags, async dispatch, cancellation, unavailable services, mock modes, and alternate routes
list side effects, artifact producers, existing tests, and observability gaps before command execution
derive nodes from stop conditions and evidence surfaces before applying reference protocols

render a self-contained report/plan.md with analysis narrative, node cut strategy, expanded node sections, variant orders, benchmark review, node-to-test coverage map, expansion strategy, and full-run readiness gate
nodeDerivationRules: create a node when a boundary changes semantic input, durable state, execution surface, side effects, dependent artifacts, async flow, or known defect risk
create or split a node when a branch can skip, block, or degrade later nodes while the current command returns success
each derived node must have a source boundary, stop condition, evidence surface, pass criteria, failure classes, first repair target, recheck standard, and advance rule
split broad phases when one command success could hide multiple independent invariants
merge only when adjacent boundaries always fail and repair together and cannot be stopped independently
referenceAlignmentRules: reference documents and domain protocols are coverage oracles, not substitutes for source analysis
compare each reference requirement as covered, split, merged, missing, not-applicable, or conditional
fill missing coverage by adding a source-derived node, focused test, or observability step before broad execution
do not mark a node passed until its own source evidence satisfies its pass criteria

granularity.planningRule: Build a code-derived chain map before applying target documents, domain overlays, or broad workflow commands.
granularity.antiPattern: Do not replace node-by-node validation with a single smoke command or one end-to-end run.
granularity.sourceBoundaryRule: Split source boundaries that can stop, fail, skip, degrade, write, persist, dispatch asynchronously, call an agent/model, or produce artifacts independently.
granularity.overlayRule: Domain overlays are optional coverage oracles selected after source analysis; they cannot replace source-derived stop conditions or pass nodes without evidence.
granularity.branchRule: Skipped, mocked, degraded, or alternate-route branches need explicit downstream impact before later nodes can be scoped or skipped.
granularity.scopeExpansionRule: Each expansion changes one variable at a time: dimensions, maxFiles, terminal toolset, provider mode, or wait behavior.

nodes.purpose: Confirm projectRoot, dataRoot, Ghost mode, runtime directory, knowledge directory, and write boundaries before running workflow checks.
passCriteria: targetProjectRoot, projectRealpath, dataRoot, runtimeDir, databasePath, knowledgeDir, skillsDir, candidatesDir, and wikiDir are absolute paths or explicitly marked n/a
isAlembicDevRepo and isExcludedProject are recorded from source checks
Alembic source repository is not used as a user runtime data root
runtime and knowledge paths are explicitly recorded
nodes.failurePolicy: Stop the run until path facts are corrected or explicit approval changes the write boundary.

nodes.purpose: Identify the workflow entry points, modules, side effects, and existing tests before changing code.
passCriteria: state transitions and side effects are summarized
focused verification command is selected before repair
nodes.failurePolicy: Split by module or state transition if the workflow boundary is still unclear.

transitionRules: pending -> running when inputs, planned commands, and evidence targets are recorded
running -> pass when all passCriteria are satisfied by evidence
running -> fail when evidence falsifies the node hypothesis and a focused repair is possible
running -> blocked when required data, approval, or safe command scope is missing
fail -> running only after recording the repair attempt and rerun command
a later broad command cannot pass an earlier node; each node needs its own source evidence
reference documents cannot pass nodes by themselves; they only reveal missing coverage or stricter criteria
a skipped, mocked, or degraded branch cannot pass downstream nodes unless the node plan explicitly scopes that branch as the target
scope expansion changes one variable at a time: dimensions, maxFiles, terminal toolset, provider mode, or wait behavior
report/plan.md must contain expanded node sections before a broad workflow command can be treated as execution-ready
async workflows must distinguish skeleton-only observation from full async execution before downstream nodes can pass
expansion nodes must change only one variable after focused validation passes
